codeReview.summary: The fix addresses the two blocking gaps from the prior failed review. In macros/src/lib.rs, #[derive(Store)] now generates explicit-id HasId and ResolveRecordId RecordIds from ModelMeta::storage_table(), and #[derive(Bridge)] hydrate dispatch now matches each payload type by storage_table() as well. Those changes align alias-backed explicit-id identity and enum foreign hydration with the repository storage identity seam, satisfying the original failure around table_as macro identity and bridge dispatch.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill now correctly points table_as work toward explicit storage identity, but the feature transcript still shows the worker had to infer a Windows-safe replacement for the jq-based milestone listing example from general environment knowledge rather than from the skill itself.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session bcb58620-7be9-40d6-a509-23eed5926e2e records a Python one-liner fallback because the skill example references jq; SKILL.md does not yet mention a Windows-safe/Python alternative for that read-only mission inspection step.

summary: Pass: commit a2ad47c routes macro-generated explicit-id identity and derive(Bridge) hydrate dispatch through storage_table(), closing the prior table_as storage identity gap in alias HasId/ResolveRecordId and enum bridge dispatch.
